mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already below its recent peak, discount rates and recession odds decline, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX returns are negative or below baseline in most easing episodes, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 while FEDFUNDS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: A policy-rate level above the neutral-to-restrictive range slows multiple asset classes at once, so when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% the next 20 trading days should skew negative for the S&P 500 through tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional average by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and demand, which should pressure cyclical earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when UNRATE is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if month-ahead SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger condition fails to improve hit rate versus a random monthly benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which should support earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint-signal buckets do not outperform single-series buckets, or if positive PAYEMS/RSAFS co-movement is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO relative to a stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies output is weakening faster than inflation, which tightens profit margins and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rejected if negative 60-day SPX returns do not cluster around periods of falling INDPRO with elevated CPIAUCSL, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts jump from depressed levels, the move signals an easing in rate-sensitive domestic demand and improves cyclical growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 8% month over month
failure_condition: Rejected if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after large HOUST jumps, or if the signal loses significance once the same-month housing move is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: In an industrial rebound regime, simultaneous gains in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicate expanding capacity use and hiring, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger revenue and earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint increase in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not improve 60-day forward returns versus either series alone, or if the effect reverses during expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already near cyclical lows and payroll growth slows, the market should interpret the labor backdrop as late-cycle exhaustion, producing weaker S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.8% and PAYEMS growth turns negative over 2 months
failure_condition: Rejected if the triggered combination fails to predict below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in a majority of late-cycle trigger instances.
